title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Autodeposit
Autodeposit allows Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
transactions to be deposited directly into customersâ accounts without the need for security questions or manual acceptance.
Blocked/Not Blocked
Not Blocked is the default state for an Interac e-Transfer transaction. A transaction is moved to a state of Blocked by Central 1 or Interac
if itâs deemed to be fraudulent or suspicious.
Blocked List
The Blocked List consists of email or mobile phone numbers Interac or Central 1 identifies as suspicious or fraudulent. Any Interac
e-Transfer transaction associated with a recipientâs email address or mobile phone number currently on a blocked list canât be completed. Blocked lists are only accessible to Interac
or Central 1. Interac or Central 1 may disable any Interac e-Transfer profile associated with an email or mobile phone number that is on a blocked list. See Disabled/Enabled.
Branch Number
The branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Disabled/Enabled
Disabled is a temporary state of a customerâs Interac
e-Transfer profile, as set by Central 1 or Interac
.
By default, an Interac e-Transfer profile is in the state of Enabled, allowing the customer to send or receive transfers through Interac e-Transfer. Profiles associated with transactions that are deemed fraudulent or suspicious, or associated with emails or mobile phone numbers currently on a blocked list, may be disabled by Interac or Central 1. Customers with disabled profiles canât send or receive transfers through Interac e-Transfer.
Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM)
Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) is a system used to monitor various transactions for suspicious activity related to fraud. Itâs comprised of a decision engine, case management and reporting or monitoring (your access may restrict what components you can see in EFM).
Expired
Expired is the status assigned by Interac and Central 1 to an Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction that wasnât deposited or cancelled within 30 days. An expired Send Money transaction canât be deposited and must be cancelled by the sender. Send Money
transactions that are expired for more than 30 days are cancelled by Interac as part of the lazy sender process.
Expired is also the status applied to an Interac e-Transfer Request Money transaction that wasnât fulfilled by the request recipient within 30 days of notification (or re-notification). An expired request money transaction canât be fulfilled. No settlement is required for expired requests since funds arenât moved when requests are sent.
Failed
Failed is a reporting designation used by Central 1 to refer to Interac
e-Transfer transactions that arenât completed or cancelled, due to one of the following:
A technical error
An Interac e-Transfer transaction has been blocked or the recipientâs email or mobile phone number is on a Blocked List
The senderâs outgoing Interac e-Transfer limit has been reached
Failed transactions appear on the ECCX (e-Transfer Failed Transactions Report).
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download and upload files from and to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Fulfill Request
A Request Money transaction can be fulfilled by any customer. Customers fulfilling requests can review the request details and agree to transfer funds to the sender of the request. They can also decline the request or prevent future requests from being received from this sender or anyone.
Institution Number
The institution number is the 3-digit number assigned by Payments Canada (CPA) that identifies the financial institution (e.g., 809 is for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number. The institution number is sometimes known as a route number.
Interac
e-Transfer Profile
An Interac
e-Transfer profile is a customer account registered with Interac that is associated with the customerâs Interac
e-Transfer transactions. The customer must register for an Interac e-Transfer profile before they are able to send or receive transfers through Interac
e-Transfer. The Interac e-Transfer profile is monitored by Central 1 and Interac and may be disabled if there is any suspicious activity.
Interac Reference Number
The Interac
Reference Number is a unique identifier that Interac
assigns to each Interac
e-Transfer transaction.
Lazy Sender Process
The lazy sender process is when Interac
e-Transfer transactions that are expired for 30 days are cancelled by Interac. If an Interac
e-Transfer hasnât been cancelled or received for a total of 60 days (30 days requested and 30 days expired), Interac will cancel the transaction within seven days. Central 1 will then process these refunds back to the sending financial institutionsâ accounts.
Limit Group
The limit group that the financial institution participates in determines the maximum dollar values that are used for Interac e-Transfer transaction limits.
No Delay Transfer
No delay transfer is a limit group feature that allows Interac to send an immediate recipient notification for pick up or deposit, if the recipientâs Autodeposit is enabled, for transactions under a certain amount and/or for transfers with a history between the Interac e-Transfer sender and recipient. Interac scores these transactions for fraud.
Retrieval Reference Number
The retrieval reference number is generated by Central 1 and is unique to each Interac
e-Transfer.
Request Money
Request Money allows the customer to request funds from a recipient through online banking. The recipient of the Request Money transaction receives an email notification from Interac
and can fulfill or decline the request. If the recipient fulfills the Request Money transaction, the recipientâs account is debited immediately and funds are deposited directly into the Request Money transaction senderâs account. The financial institution must be subscribed to Enhanced User Features for its customers to initiate or fulfill Request Money transactions.
Requested
Requested is the status assigned by Central 1 for an Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction that hasnât been received or cancelled. An Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction can be requested for up to 30 days. After 30 days, the status of the Interac
e-Transfer changes to expired.
Rolling Limit
A rolling limit is the maximum total dollar amount of Interac
e-Transfer transactions that a customer can send within a rolling time period.
Rolling Time Period
A rolling time period is the time that is calculated from the moment of entering an Interac e-Transfer transaction, to a moment back in time, by a pre-defined number of hours or days. The total amounts of a customerâs completed Interac
e-Transfer outgoing transactions occurring during this period determines the rolling limits that the current Interac e-Transfer transaction will be assessed against. Current definitions of rolling time periods include 24-hour, 7-day, and 30-day periods.
For example, assume that a $3,000 rolling limit for a 24-hour rolling time period for Interac Send Money transactions applies to a financial institutionâs customers. Assume also that yesterday a customer had Interac e-Transfer Send Money transactions as follows:
$1,000 at 11:00 AM
$1,000 at 01:00 PM
$1,000 at 05:00 PM
The limit on Send Money transactions that the customer can send at sample selected times today, based on the rolling time period in effect at each time, and on the amount of Interac e-Transfer Send Money transactions completed in that period, would be as follows:
Time
Rolling Time Period in Effect
Rolling Limit Used
Remaining Limit
9:00 AM
Yesterday 9:00 AM â Today 9:00 AM
$3,000
$0
12:00 AM
Yesterday 12:00 AM â Today 12:00 AM
$2,000
$1,000
02:00 PM
Yesterday 02:00 PM â Today 02:00 PM
$1,000
$2,000
06:00 PM
Yesterday 06:00 PM â Today 06:00 PM
$0
$3,000
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Transit Number
The transit number is made up of a leading zero followed by the institution number together with the branch number (e.g., 080912010, 082812010).
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Question 1
Is it mandatory to participate in limit groups?
Answer
Limit groups are an optional feature of Interac e-Transfer. Your financial institution can use the existing e-Transfer limits if you prefer.
Question 2
What are the different limits of the limit groups?
Answer
The details of each limit group are provided in Section 4.2, Limit Groups.
Question 3
My banking system has limits for Interac e-Transfer transactions. Can I keep them?
Answer
Some banking systems may allow you to apply lower-level limits than the ones that are set by Central 1 at Interac.
Outgoing transactions:
You can offer your customers the limit ceiling that Central 1 sets, or you can apply a lower limit at your host banking system. You can choose a different ceiling by participating in a limit group and still maintain lower-level limits at the banking system while accessing the no delay transfer. Managing the send limits on the banking system provides you with the greatest flexibility to assign and manage limits appropriate to your customer/business.
Incoming transactions:
Incoming limits at the banking system must support an industry-wide per transaction limit of $25,000.
Note: Financial institutions can continue to apply a lower outgoing limit at an individual customer level to manage risk of fraud or AML.
Question 4
Are there any banking system changes required to implement limit groups?
Answer
Central 1 must update its communication with Interac to identify the limit group that applies to e-Transfers for your financial institution. If you set e-Transfer limits in your host banking system that are different from the ceilings set in the limit group that you select, you must review these changes with your banking host to determine the impact.
Additionally, to support limit groups, Interac requires the customer/business name in its communication with Central 1. Central 1 uses the customer/business name in the Customer Profile message passed from the banking system in its communication with Central 1. If your banking system does not support the Customer Profile message, contact your Central 1  Account Executive to discuss options.
Question 5
How should we market no delay transfer to our customers?
Answer
Interac has advised that information related to the no delay transfer feature is sensitive and should only be shared with staff supporting the Interac e-Transfer service. We recommend that you donât share any information about how the no delay transfer feature works with your customers.
Question 6
How can we offer higher Request Money limits?
Answer
Currently most limit groups have a maximum Request Money limit of $3,000, which is supported throughout the industry. If your financial institution would like to offer higher Request Money limits, please contact your Central 1  Account Executive.
END
